title: Meehania faberi
taxonomy: Meehania faberi is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is a member of the genus Meehania, which is a group of plants native to East Asia. The plant is further classified as a member of the subfamily Lamioideae, which includes many species of mint and basil. Meehania faberi is also known to be closely related to the genus Nepeta and the genus Prunella. Through its classification, Meehania faberi is able to be identified and placed among other similar plants in the botanical world.

intro: Meehania faberi, also known as Faber's Meehania, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to China and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Lamiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Meehania faberi include phenolic compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, as well as alkaloids and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Meehania faberi is commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine and can be found in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures.
